content.full_text:
February 7, 1977


Ms. Carol J. Stick
Registrar of Voters
County of Sacramento
Department of Voter Registration
and Elections
3700 Branch Center Road, Suite C
Sacramento, CA  95827

Dear Ms. Stick:

     This letter will confirm my telephone conversations with
you and Ms. Dixie Blagy on February 4, 1977. In those
conversations, I provided advice regarding your December 10,
1976 letter relating to your duties as a filing officer who
receives original campaign disclosure statements.

     The question we discussed is summarized as follows:

          Pursuant to Government Code Section 91613(a), are
          you required to fine Mr. Hollis E. Barnes, a
          candidate for Director of the Sacramento Area
          Economic Opportunity Council?

     The essential facts underlying this question are as follows:

          1.  The campaign disclosure statement was due
               September 23, 1976.

          2.  You put a specific written notice in the mail
               on September 28, 1976, but the notice was postmarked
               September 29, 1976.

          3.  Mr. Barnes received the notification on Friday,
               October 1, 1976.

          4.  Mr. Barnes mailed his statement (Form 470) on
               Sunday, October 3, 1976, but the statement was
               postmarked and received by you on October 4, 1976.

     Based on these facts, you are not required to fine Mr. Barnes
for late filing. Rather, you may use your discretion to determine
whether or not Mr. Barnes should be fined.

Ms. Carol J. Stick
February 7, 1977
Page Two

You are not required to fine Mr. Barnes pursuant to
Government Code Section 91013(a) because the date you mailed
specific written notification is September 29, 1976--the date
your notice was postmarked. And because Mr. Barnes filed his
statement on October 4, 1976, he filed within five days after
you sent your notice.

I hope the above advice is helpful. If you have any
questions regarding this advice, please contact me.

Sincerely,


John C. Greenwood
Program Coordinator


JCG:av

COUNTY OF SACRAMENTO
DEPARTMENT OF VOTER REGISTRATION & ELECTIONS
3700 BRANCH CENTER ROAD, SUITE C
SACRAMENTO CALIFORNIA 95827
(916) 440-9611

CAROL J. STACK
REGISTRAR OF VOTERS

DECEMBER 10, 1976

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 K STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

ATTENTION: REGINA KUTCHINS

DEAR MS. KUTCHINS:

I RECEIVED THE ATTACHED LETTER FROM MR. HOLLIS E. BARNES, A CANDIDATE FOR DIRECTOR
OF THE SACRAMENTO AREA ECONOMIC OPPORTUNITY COUNCIL. HE DID COME TO THIS OFFICE
PRIOR TO WRITING THE LETTER REQUESTING CONSULTATION WITH COUNTY COUNSEL AND FAIR
POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION AND I GAVE HIM THE FOLLOWING VERBAL REPLIES TO HIS
QUESTIONS.

QUESTION #1. SHOULD NOT THE 5 DAYS TO COMPLY AFTER NOTIFICATION BEGIN AND END
USING THE SAME CRITERIA, I.E., DATE OF PREPARATION OF YOUR
NOTIFICATION AND MY STATEMENT, DATE OF THE MAILING OF YOUR
NOTIFICATION AND MY MAILING OF MY STATEMENT OR DATE OF THE RECEIPT
OF YOUR NOTIFICATION AND MY STATEMENT.

ANSWER      I QUOTED GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 91013(a) ESPECIALLY INDICATING
THAT NO LIABILITY SHALL BE WAIVED IF A STATEMENT OR REPORT IS NOT
FILED WITHIN FIVE DAYS AFTER THE FILING OFFICER HAS SENT SPECIFIC
WRITTEN NOTICE OF THE FILING REQUIREMENT. MY LETTER DATED SEPTEMBER 28,
1976 WAS PUT IN THE MAIL ON THE SAME DATE BUT APPARENTLY OUR MESSAGE
CENTER DID NOT POSTMARK IT UNTIL SEPTEMBER 29, 1976 (COPY OF LETTER
ATTACHED). MY INTERPRETATION OF THE CODE IS THAT THE FIVE DAYS
GRACE BEGINS ON THE DATE THAT THE LETTER WAS DATED AND MAILED
IRREGARDLESS OF THE POSTMARK, AND THAT THE CANDIDATE MUST FILE BY THE
END OF THE GRACE PERIOD WHICH WAS OCTOBER 2, 1976 IN ORDER TO AVOID
PENALTIES.

QUESTION #2. WHAT EFFECT DOES WEEKENDS HAVE ON COMPLYING WITH THE DEADLINE.
BECAUSE OF THE DAY AND TIME I RECEIVED THE NOTIFICATION AND THE
DEADLINE IT WAS IMPOSSIBLE TO COMPLY.

ANSWER      I INDICATED TO HIM THAT WEEKENDS COUNT AS PART OF THE FIVE DAYS
GRACE PERIOD. I INFORMED HIM THAT WHEN HE SIGNED FOR HIS NOMINATION
PACKAGE THAT THE CAMPAIGN REPORTING WAS POINTED OUT AS A REQUIREMENT
AND THE INFORMATION MANUAL ON CAMPAIGN DISCLOSURE WAS ISSUED TO HIM.
IT WAS HIS RESPONSIBILITY TO SEE THAT THE REPORT WAS FILED IN A
TIMELY MANNER. THE MANUAL CLEARLY STATES ON PAGE 4 "STATEMENTS MUST
BE RECEIVED BY THE APPROPRIATE FILING OFFICER BY THE FILING DEADLINE.
IT IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO SEND THE STATEMENTS BY ORDINARY MAIL POST-
MARKED AS OF THE FILING DEADLINE. HOWEVER, STATEMENTS MAILED

FIRST-CLASS REGISTERED MAIL (NOT CERTIFIED MAIL) WILL BE CONSIDERED
RECEIVED ON THE DATE OF THE POSTMARK."

QUESTION #3. SHOULD ANY PENALTY ASSESSED BE IN ACCORDANCE WITH SUBSECTION (B) OF
GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 91013 RATHER THAN SUBSECTION (A).

ANSWER          NO. THE CANDIDATE WAS REQUIRED TO FILE AN ORIGINAL STATEMENT WITH
SACRAMENTO COUNTY BECAUSE HE WAS A LOCAL CANDIDATE.

REQUEST YOUR ADVICE AND COUNSEL ON THE ABOVE ISSUES.

VERY TRULY YOURS,

Carol J. Stick

CAROL J. STICK
REGISTRAR OF VOTERS

ENCLOSURES
CC: HOLLIS BARNES

"Massey action"
"Claim"

7/1 Oct 76

Dear Mrs. Stick

I would like to request a review of the
penalty assessed in your letter to me dtd 10-7-76.
BACKGROUND:

I failed to file a campaign statement prior
to the 7-23-76 deadline.

On 9-28-76 you mailed a letter to me
reminding me of the filing deadline. It was postmarked
9-29-76 and received by me other week on Fri.
10-1-76.

On 10-2-76 I prepared a campaign statement.
This was mailed by me on 10-3-76. The letter was
postmarked 10-4-76.

On 10-7-76 you concluded that I shall be
penalized 100$ for failing to comply with the 10-23-76
deadline & your subsequent reminder. (postcard at 10^8 pm
day from 29 Sep. to 3 Oct.)

QUESTIONS:

1. Show me the 5 days to comply after
notification begin if you'd using the same criteria & date
of preparation of your notification & my statement, date
of the mailing of your notification & my mailing of my
statement and or date of the receipt of your notification & my
statement.

2. What effect day week-end have on computing [illegible]

with the deadline. Because of the lay off time, I received merit the mortician and the deadline it was impossible to comply.

2. Showed any possibly assessed bill accordance with Subsection B of Golf Code Jo. 97013 rather than Subsection A.

RECOMMENDATION: That the District Attorney, County Counsel and/or from Political Bodies Commission be consulted regarding answers to the above questions.

Thank you for your consideration of this matter.

Sincerely

[signature]
Thos. E. ERNEST
Coordinator for SAFOC.

COUNTY OF SACRAMENTO
DEPARTMENT OF VOTER REGISTRATION & ELECTIONS
3700 BRANCH CENTER ROAD, SUITE C
SACRAMENTO CALIFORNIA 95827
(916) 440-5611

CAROL J. STICK
REGISTRAR OF VOTERS

OCTOBER 7, 1976

HOLLIS E. BARNES
7653 BETH ST
SACRAMENTO, CA 95832

RE: CAMPAIGN STATEMENT - LATE FILING FEE

I NOTIFIED YOU ON SEPTEMBER 28, 1976 THAT YOUR CAMPAIGN STATEMENT DUE NO
LATER THAN SEPTEMBER 23, 1976 HAD NOT BEEN FILED.

YOUR STATEMENT WAS FILED ON OCTOBER 4, 1976.

GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 91013 REQUIRES THAT PENALTIES ARE MANDATORY FOR REPORTS
RECEIVED STARTING FIVE DAYS AFTER THE FILING OFFICER HAS SENT SPECIFIC NOTICE OF
THE FILING REQUIREMENT AND UNTIL THE STATEMENT IS FILED.

I AM SORRY TO INFORM YOU THAT A PENALTY IN THE AMOUNT OF ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($100.00)
HAS BEEN ASSESSED. THIS AMOUNT IS NOW DUE AND PAYABLE. YOUR CHECK OR MONEY
ORDER SHOULD BE PAYABLE TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY AND MAILED TO THE LETTERHEAD
ADDRESS.

VERY TRULY YOURS,

CAROL J. STICK
REGISTRAR OF VOTERS

DDB/MA

CANDIDATES CAMPAIGN STATEMENT

SHORT FORM

(GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 84200-84214)

Period 1/1/76 through 2/29/76

FILED
OCT 4 1976

BY CAROL J. STICK, REGISTRAR
FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY
DEPUTY

A

B

A candidate for whom not more than $200 has been received or spent on behalf of his candidacy may file this short form.
NOTE: Once contributions or expenditures exceed $200 for the ENTIRE CAMPAIGN, the candidate must file Form 430.

Name of candidate _____ Hollis E. Barnes

Residential address 7655 IBETH H. Sacramento, Calif. 95832 Phone 916 1465-7829
(NO. AND STREET) (CITY) (STATE) (ZIP CODE) (AREA CODE)

Business address 7801 Lode BLVD Sacramento,CA Calif. Phone (714) 363-3161
(NO. AND STREET) (CITY) (STATE) (ZIP CODE) (AREA CODE)

Type of election _____ General Date of election Nov. 2, 1976
(PRIMARY GENERAL SPECIAL) (MONTH, DAY, YEAR)

Office for which you are a candidate ____ Sacrament Area Economic Opportunity Council

Political party and district number (if applicable) _____ N/A

VERIFICATION

I declare under penalty of perjury that to the best of my knowledge not more than $200 has been received or expended on behalf of or
in support of my candidacy, by myself or by any committee of which I have knowledge.

Executed on 2 Oct. 76 at Sacramento, Calif
(DATE) (CITY AND STATE)

[SIGNATURE OF CANDIDATE]

Registration in Fratribus
320 Say a Ceert pl
Savannah Ga 31401

N.E. BURIED
723 BIRTH ST
Savannah Ga 31552

SENDA - [illegible] on it and
return to [illegible] on the
[illegible]

"TURN TO" space on

1. The following service is requested (check one).

□ Show to whom and date delivered.............. 12¢
☑ Show to whom, date, & address of delivery.. 35¢
☑ DELIVER ONLY TO ADDRESSEE and
show to whom and date delivered........... 65¢
□ DELIVER ONLY TO ADDRESSEE and
show to whom, date, and address of
delivery................................ 85¢

2. ARTICLE ADDRESSED TO:

LLOYDS BANK
695 FIFTH AV
NEW YORK

3. ARTICLE DESCRIPTION:

REGISTERED NO.: CERTIFIED NO.: INSURED NO.

[illegible] NO.

(Addressee obtain signature of addressee or agent)

4. I have received the article described above.

SIGNATURE

4. DATE OF DELIVERY

MAY 1 1968 [POSTMARK]

5. ADDRESS (Complete only if requested)

[illegible]

6. UNABLE TO DELIVER BECAUSE: | CLERK'S
                                | INITIALS

* GPO 1968 0 - 527-897

UNITED STATES POSTAL SERVICE
OFFICIAL BUSINESS

PENALTY FOR PRIVATE USE $300

[illegible box with postal markings]

RETURN TO

Forgiving Enough

900 FRANCIE C
SACRAMENTO, CA 95827

[stamp/seal in upper left corner]

[postal markings and cancellations visible]

COUNTY OF SACRAMENTO
DEPARTMENT OF VOTER REGISTRATION & ELECTIONS
1130 BRANCH CENTER ROAD, SUITE C
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95827
(916) 440-6571

CAROL J. STICK
REGISTRAR OF VOTERS

SEPTEMBER 28, 1976

HOLLIS E. BARNES
7693 BETH STREET
SACRAMENTO, CA 95852

PURSUANT TO THE POLITICAL REFORM INITIATIVE (PROPOSITION 9) ALL OFFICEHOLDERS,
CANDIDATES, COMMITTEE AND MAJOR DONORS FOR STATE OR LOCAL PUBLIC OFFICE MUST
FILE CAMPAIGN STATEMENTS ITEMIZING RECEIPTS AND EXPENDITURES. AS AN OFFICEHOLDER,
CANDIDATE, COMMITTEE, OR MAJOR DONOR, YOU WERE REQUIRED TO FILE A CAMPAIGN
STATEMENT NO LATER THAN __________ TO COVER THE PERIOD
__________ THROUGH __________

AS OF THIS DATE, YOUR STATEMENT WAS NOT BEEN RECEIVED. GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION
91013, PROVIDES AS FOLLOWS:

(A) IF ANY PERSON FILES AN ORIGINAL STATEMENT OR REPORT, AFTER ANY DEADLINE
IMPOSED BY THIS ACT, SHALL, IN ADDITION TO ANY OTHER PENALTIES OR REMEDIES
ESTABLISHED BY THIS ACT, BE LIABLE IN THE AMOUNT OF TEN DOLLARS ($10) PER DAY AFTER
THE DEADLINE UNTIL THE STATEMENT OR REPORT IS FILED, TO THE OFFICER WITH WHOM
STATEMENT OR REPORT IS REQUIRED TO BE FILED. LIABILITY NEED NOT BE ENFORCED BY
THE FILING OFFICER IF HE IS ON AN IMPARTIAL BASIS HE DETERMINES THAT THE LATE FILING WAS
NOT WILLFUL AND THAT ENFORCEMENT OF THE LIABILITY WILL NOT FURTHER THE PURPOSES OF
THE ACT, EXCEPT THAT NO LIABILITY SHALL BE WAIVED IF A STATEMENT OR REPORT IS NOT
FILED WITHIN FIVE DAYS AFTER THE FILING OFFICER HAS SENT SPECIFIC WRITTEN NOTICE
OF THE FILING REQUIREMENT.

(B) IF ANY PERSON FILES A COPY OF A STATEMENT OR REPORT AFTER ANY DEADLINE
IMPOSED BY THIS ACT, HE SHALL, IN ADDITION TO ANY OTHER PENALTIES OR REMEDIES
ESTABLISHED BY THIS CHAPTER, BE LIABLE IN THE AMOUNT OF TEN DOLLARS ($10) PER DAY,
STARTING FIVE DAYS AFTER THE OFFICER HAS SENT SPECIFIC NOTICE OF THE FILING
REQUIREMENT AND UNTIL THE STATEMENT IS FILED.

(C) THE OFFICER SHALL DEPOSIT ANY FUNDS RECEIVED UNDER THIS SECTION INTO
THE GENERAL FUND OF THE JURISDICTION OF WHICH HE IS AN OFFICER. NO LIABILITY UNDER
THIS SECTION SHALL EXCEED THE CUMULATIVE AMOUNT STATED IN THE LATE STATEMENT OR
REPORT, OR ONE HUNDRED DOLLARS ($100), WHICHEVER IS GREATER."

ACCORDINGLY, THE PENALTY PROVIDED IN _____(A)_____ ABOVE WILL BEGIN TO ACCRUE
IF THE STATEMENT IS NOT FILED BY OCTOBER 2, 1976 .

SECTION 91010 OF THE CALIFORNIA GOVERNMENT CODE REQUIRES THE FILING OFFICER
TO DETERMINE THAT A TIMELY FILING HAS BEEN MADE AND THAT ALL ITEMS ON EACH FORM
HAVE BEEN COMPLETED.

THE LAW ALSO REQUIRES THIS OFFICE TO REPORT APPARENT VIOLATIONS TO THE DISTRICT
ATTORNEY.

PLEASE CALL THE CAMPAIGN REPORTING UNIT AT 440-3641 IF YOU REQUIRE FURTHER
INFORMATION.

CC: DISTRICT ATTORNEY                    SINCERELY,
    COUNTY COUNSEL
                                        Carol J. Stick
                                        CAROL J. STICK

RECEIPT FOR CERTIFIED MAIL—30¢ (plus postage)

SENT TO: HILLIS BARNES
         945 BETH ST
         
STREET AND NO.

P.O., STATE AND ZIP CODE     HC     CA 95631

OPTIONAL SERVICES FOR ADDITIONAL FEES

RETURN          1. Shown to whom and date delivered      15¢
RECEIPT         2. Shown to whom, date and where delivered
                   with restricted delivery              65¢
SERVICES        RESTRICTED DELIVERY                      85¢
SPECIAL DELIVERY (extra fee required)                     50¢

Form          NO INSURANCE COVERAGE PROVIDED—          [See other side]
Blue 1970-3800  NOT FOR PERSONAL MAIL

No. _______________

[illegible] on left margin

sections.question:
1. Should not the 5 days to comply after notification begin & end using the same criteria i.e. date of preparation of your notification & my statement, date of the mailing of your notification & my mailing of my statement and or date of the receipt of your notification & my statement.

2. What effect does work-week have on complying

with the deadline. Because of the day & time I received the notification and the deadline it was impossible to comply.

3. Should any penalty assessed be in accordance with Subsection B of Govt Code Sec. 91013 rather than subsection A.

RECOMMENDATION: That the District Attorney, County Counsel and/or Fair Political Practices Commission be consulted regarding answers to the above questions.

Thank you for your consideration of this matter.
sections.facts:
I failed to file a campaign statement prior to the 9-23-76 deadline.

On 9-28-76 you prepared a letter to me reminding me of the filing deadline. It was postmarked 9-29-76 and received by me after work on Fri 10-1-76.

On 10-2-76 I prepared a campaign statement. This was mailed by me on 10-3-76. The letter was postmarked 10-4-76.

On 10-7-76 you concluded that I should be penalized $100 for failing to comply with the 9-23-76 deadline & your subsequent reminder. (Assessed at 10^3 per day from 24 Sep. to 3 Oct.).
